body: Will fix the clamp values - testing on real iPhone SE now. Should be ready by end of week.
Good, the headline wraps fine but the tagline 'Books worth your time' still clips. I've attached a screenshot from my phone.
Confirmed - Stripe dashboard was still on USD from the test account. Switching currency and updating tax settings to Swedish VAT now.
Verify the VAT rate is 6% for books, not 25% - Swedish books use the reduced cultural rate.
The signup form looks good on desktop. Please check: does it also show the GDPR consent checkbox? That's mandatory.
Yes, consent checkbox is in - labelled 'Jag godkänner att Weaver & Book lagrar min e-postadress'. I'll add the privacy policy link too.
Using Cookieyes for this - free plan covers what we need. Will configure reject-all as the default action.
Fine with CookieYes as long as it is CNIL / IMY compliant. Send me the preview link when done.
This is a Webflow publish-time setting. I have updated the custom domain canonical in project settings to https://www.weaverandbook.se.
I can write the alt text in Swedish - send me the image list and I will fill them in. Maja can also provide captions.
Gallery image list shared in the design folder. There are 14 images in total, and I have added draft alt text for the 3 non-window-display ones already.
The Contentful rich-text renderer library handles Markdown-to-richtext cleanly. I'll use @contentful/rich-text-types for the schema.
I will draft the updated about page copy this week and paste it into the Webflow CMS editor directly - no need to go through the script pipeline for this one.
I can add a Sharp step to the sync pipeline - resizes to 1200px wide and outputs WebP. Will test against Maja's latest batch.
Good. Make sure the event poster crops maintain the poster aspect ratio - do not centre-crop, add letterbox if needed.
Tested the webhook locally with Stripe CLI - payment_intent.succeeded fires and the SendGrid template email arrived. Ready to merge.
Added try/except around the ISBN field access with entry.get('isbn', '') fallback. Re-ran against the full Contentful export - no crashes.
Confirm the image in og:image points to the bookshop exterior photo Maja took in March, not the Webflow default. The URL must be absolute HTTPS.
Lighthouse score after your fix is now 91 on mobile. Acceptable. The remaining LCP cost is the hero image which is above-fold and should stay eager-loaded.
Date format looks correct now in staging. Instance: 12 mars 2026 on the Nora Clement reading night listing.
